steps.title: Identify the vertex
steps.content: The object reaches its maximum height of 268 feet at $t=3$ seconds, so the vertex of the parabola is $(h,k)=(3,268)$.

steps.title: Write the vertex form
steps.content: The vertex form is $$f(t)=a(t-h)^{2}+k.$$ Substituting $h=3$, $k=268$, and the standard projectile coefficient $a=-16$ (feet, downward) gives $$f(t)=-16(t-3)^{2}+268.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(t)=-16(t+268)^{2}-3$ — vertex would be $(-268,-3)$, not $(3,268)$. Eliminate.
  • B) $f(t)=-16(t-268)^{2}+3$ — vertex would be $(268,3)$, not $(3,268)$. Eliminate.
  • C) $f(t)=-16(t+3)^{2}-268$ — vertex would be $(-3,-268)$, a minimum below ground. Eliminate.
  • D) $f(t)=-16(t-3)^{2}+268$ — vertex $(3,268)$ and $f(0)=124$. Keep.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
